panel.title: Rally Annotator
status.ready: Ready. Pick the Sport, then mark rallies. Click Help for usage.
status.noMediaStart: No media playing — cannot mark START.
status.unsavedGuard: You have an UNSAVED rally (START → END). Click 'Save Rally' to keep it, or 'Undo last' to clear it, before marking a new START.
status.startSet: START set @ {t}s. Play to the rally's end, then Mark END.
status.noMediaEnd: No media playing — cannot mark END.
status.endSet: END set @ {t}s. Choose the Ending reason, then click Save Rally.
status.needStart: Set a START time first (click Mark START).
status.needEnd: Set an END time first (click Mark END).
status.zeroLength: END must be later than START (rally must be > 0s).
status.duplicate: Rally #{n} already exists — set "Next #" to a free number.
status.needSelectEdit: Pick a rally in the Recent list first, then Edit.
status.editGuard: Finish the current rally ('Save Rally') or clear it ('Undo last') before editing another — your unsaved START → END would be lost.
status.editing: Editing rally #{n}. Adjust Start/End/reason, then Save changes. (Undo cancels.)
status.needSelectDelete: Pick a rally in the Recent list first, then Delete.
status.clearedMark: Cleared the in-progress START/END (nothing was written).
status.noVideoFound: No video found on this page yet.
status.deletedHint: (click Download CSV to refresh the file)
status.modeNew: Mode: new rally (Mark START, Mark END, reason, Save Rally).
help.html:
Rally Annotator (web) — how to use
1. Pick the Sport (stays set).
2. Back 5s / Play / Pause / Fwd 5s drive the page's video from here.
3. At a rally's start click Mark START (pause/scrub first for accuracy); at its end click Mark END. Fine-tune the Start/End seconds by editing the fields.
4. Choose the Ending reason (or leave it unknown), optionally type a Number of shots, then Save Rally — the full CSV downloads.
5. Reason & shots RESET after each save (never silently reused); Sport stays.
6. Recent rallies: click a row, then Edit / Delete. Undo last removes the most recent row, clears an in-progress mark, or cancels an edit.
7. Rallies autosave to extension storage keyed to this video, so reloading the page resumes numbering. The CSV lands in your Downloads folder (it can't be written next to a web video).

The ending-reason values (winner / forced_error / unforced_error / service_fault / let / other / unknown) are written to the CSV in canonical English regardless of the interface language.
